reviews.author: Matthew Gray
reviews.text: An interesting and authentic stop... gritty, raw, and for some, possibly confronting. The live / wet market food options, local stalls, and narrow paths give a real glimpse into daily life far removed from the polished tourist zones. The views over the valley are absolutely spectacular, even if the whole place feels precariously perched on the mountainside. As a local pitstop on the ride to somewhere else, it’s an eye-opening and memorable window into a very different rhythm of life.

reviews.author: Leor Nevo
reviews.text: Amazing attrraction but you need to be reasonable fotness to climb 300 stairs beyond the 305 meter elevator quite challanging extream attractions at the top...

reviews.author: Michael Case
reviews.text: Stopped here for a break on the long trip from Hanoi to Mai Chau. Had a clear view of the beautiful Mai Chau Valley. Really enjoyed wandering around looking at the activities of the road side stalls. Saw a motorcycle pull up carrying a large quantity of oranges for sale.

reviews.author: Thuha Ng
reviews.text: This is not an exactly stop for drivers but you can take a short break and have a quite good view after driving a long saddle. In this place, they often offer corn, egg and some local foods. This road is dangerous in the early morning or late afternoon because of thick foggy.

reviews.author: Linh Vương
reviews.text: This is not an actual view point, it is a stop on the road with stores sell cơm lam, thịt nướng and trứng nướng.

reviews.author: Anh Nguyen Duc
reviews.text: Beautiful view and delicious snack .interesting to break time on the trip

reviews.author: Josef Schneckenreither
reviews.text: Nice drive but nothing to get excited about. All the Roadside Shops are fully commercial and nothing left of the "Traditional Spirit" just a big con - rip-off. 5 years back I truly enjoyed it, so I come back with my wife, but very disappointing now

reviews.author: Roma Rusetsky
reviews.text: A great view is definitely worth the drive, would check the weather.

reviews.author: Chica Gamer
reviews.text: The view is so nice. It's on the side way of the road. Local people also work beyond there.

reviews.author: Phuckyu Googlescumbaggs
reviews.text: This location maybe part of the mountain pass, but it's not the official stopping point. However, at this section, you can actually see the Deo Da Trang viewpoint (which attracts fees to use the vantage points). This section has a few food and coffee shops as well as fruit/vegetable vendors etc.. The cafes and food shops have some great views, but of course, you have to use their services to get access to any unobstructed views of the scenery of Tan Lac and Xom Bay. I was on a motorbike, so I just pulled up at a spot on the highway, close to the barrier railing where there are clear views and took a video and some photos..... and all for free. (No, I'm not thrifty or a cheap-skate, I actually stopped at Deo Da Trang first and bought a caphe muoi.)

reviews.author: Halcolt Sync
reviews.text: Nice meat but quite expensive, 20k or 0.8$ is quite alot but since this is middlw of nowhere, i guess it is ok

reviews.author: Mohamed EL JAAFARI
reviews.text: Such a unique place, and great personal experience. The people are so genuine and nice, it was one of our most incredible experiences in Vietnam; and the views are so amazing.

reviews.author: Nguyễn Linh
reviews.text: Nice stop on the way Hanoi - Mai Chau. The valley's view from the pass is really nice, Fresh local fruits & food are sold by local ethnic people along the way. However, it is hard to find a standard WC there.

reviews.author: Chenghun Phukon
reviews.text: This will come on the way to Mai Chau. You can find shops around where you can buy or have your breakfast with the beautiful view of the mountains and hills. It's so relaxing there.

reviews.author: Trần Hoàng Anh
reviews.text: Great place for short break, very nice view

reviews.author: Ivan Szilva
reviews.text: Im not sure what the whole fuss is about. We drove there, but didnt even make a picture I could upload to this review, since the view is not special at all. You can see way nicer scenary in Pu Long.

reviews.author: Sophie Loca
reviews.text: Beautiful view, we stopped for a coffee and lounged a while in the cafe, it was nice.

reviews.author: Lâm Hoàng Vinh
reviews.text: Free entrance, tolet fee 3k/pax

reviews.author: Valentin Szabo
reviews.text: Was expecting something a bit more ... just some dirt road stops with dodgy shops and foodstalls no real wievpoint . Almost 3 hts from hanoi reconsider choosing this as a day trip

reviews.author: Tuan Anh Hoang (Hai Phong Trip)
reviews.text: A stopover along National road No. 6, a must stop when travelling through Thung Khe pass in Hoa Binh province. Lots of local things and local culture to see and experiences.

reviews.author: thang Van
reviews.text: Landscape very nicely, transportation need more carefully during travelling

reviews.author: Sophie Browne
reviews.text: One of my favourite places and memories, incredible view point. Great view of Vietnam.

reviews.author: Quentin Chateau
reviews.text: Beautiful. But this is just a pass, your bus driver will probably just stop there for you, not need to plan on going there.

reviews.author: Lior Dorot
reviews.text: Amazing scenery, no food to eat except to boiled corn

reviews.author: Chi Nguyen
reviews.text: Reststop with great views and local foods. Should experience.

reviews.author: Tuân Lê
reviews.text: Best place for taking photo. Beautiful white rock mountain

reviews.author: Trung Nguyen Tuan
reviews.text: Great view on the way to Moc Chau and Puluong.

reviews.author: Nhan Le
reviews.text: Great view, 4 star because shops partially block the view
